title: albuca villosa subsp. villosa
intro: Albuca villosa subsp. villosa, also known as the woolly leaved white arum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has various health benefits, such as treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its alkaloid and saponin content. The herbal preparations of Albuca villosa subsp. villosa typically involve drying the plant material, followed by decoction or infusion, to utilize its medicinal properties.
